label: sandbox • background job lifecycle: start, poll, kill, verify
query: Start the long-running command 'sleep 300' in your sandbox as a background job (sandbox_exec with background:true). Use sandbox_process to confirm it is actually running, then kill it, then confirm it is gone. Finally write a short lifecycle log of what you observed to bg_lifecycle.txt in your home — include the pid, the running confirmation, and the word 'killed'.
notes: Full background lifecycle through the sanctioned surface: background:true (not shell '&'), sandbox_process poll + kill. Requires backgroundProcessEnabled; the lifecycle file pins that each stage was actually observed. The 'killed' check is case-insensitive: the query is a natural-language contract ('include the word'), and a log that reads 'Status: Killed' has observed the same lifecycle stage — sentence capitalization is not a lifecycle defect (12B failed only on the capital K while every stage was genuinely proven via sandbox_process).
